changes_made.description: Reduced 432-line generic skill to ~80 lines focused on task-relevant commands (branches, cherry-pick, tags, history analysis). Added rules about writing output files to workspace root and careful counting.
Added --all flag and awk-based alternative for merge commit detection. Model did not load skill this run.
Switched to git -C <repo> pattern to avoid cd-ing into subdirectories. Added explicit write_file guidance. Model did not load skill.
Packed critical rules into skill description field (always visible in system prompt). Caused model to generate 65536 tokens and timeout.
Reverted to iteration 1 approach with improved merge detection using awk-based parent counting. Model did not load skill this run.

key_findings: devstral-small inconsistently loads skills via <load-skill> mechanism - the bare agent requires models to explicitly request skill loading
When the skill IS loaded, it significantly helps (0 -> 80 on git-log-analyze)
The primary failure mode is writing output files inside the repo subdirectory instead of workspace root
Putting instructions in the skill description (always visible) caused the model to generate excessive output and timeout
git-branch-ops consistently scores 100/100 regardless of skill loading, suggesting the model handles branch/cherry-pick/tag operations well natively
